text.title: Grafana — Basics Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules including a `skip-this-methodology` rule. Each pairs a binary check with sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Dashboards MUST be authored as code (JSON or Grafonnet) and version-controlled. UI-clicked dashboards are rejected (drift, no audit, no review).
rule.rationale: UI dashboards drift, get deleted, can't be reviewed; code-as-source enables PRs + diffs + rollback.

rule.statement: Data sources MUST be provisioned via files (provisioning/datasources/*.yaml) or API, never created interactively. Anonymous interactive creates rejected.
rule.rationale: Manual data sources break when Grafana is rebuilt; provisioned sources are reproducible.

rule.statement: Alert rules MUST be authored as files (Grafana unified alerting YAML) and version-controlled. UI-only alerts are rejected.
rule.rationale: UI alerts drift silently; YAML alerts are reviewed and versioned.

rule.statement: Dashboards MUST live in folders aligned with team/product structure with RBAC scoped per folder. Flat root-folder dashboards are rejected for >10-dashboard installations.
rule.rationale: Flat dashboards become unfindable; folder + RBAC makes ownership explicit.

rule.statement: Public dashboard links MUST be off by default; explicit allowlist of dashboards may be opened with documented risk acceptance.
rule.rationale: Public links leak internal data; default-off + allowlist is the only safe shape.

rule.statement: Skip when (a) single ad-hoc dashboard with no maintenance need OR (b) Grafana is not the viewer of record.
rule.rationale: Methodology overhead requires Grafana + maintenance horizon; without them it's ceremony.
